questions.question: Explain the differences between Google Compute Engine, Google App Engine, and Google Kubernetes Engine. When would you choose each for a cloud deployment?
keyPoints: Compute Engine: IaaS, virtual machines with full control
App Engine: PaaS, managed runtime for applications
Kubernetes Engine: CaaS, container orchestration with Kubernetes
Selection criteria: control vs. management tradeoff, scaling requirements

questions.question: Describe the architecture of Google BigQuery. How does it achieve fast query performance on large datasets, and what are its key features for data warehousing?
keyPoints: Columnar storage with Dremel query engine architecture
Separate storage and compute for cost efficiency
Supports SQL queries, streaming inserts, and federated data sources

questions.question: Design a cloud architecture for a full-stack web application that requires: (a) user authentication and authorization, (b) a relational database for transactional data, (c) object storage for media files, (d) real-time analytics on user behavior, and (e) autoscaling based on traffic. Specify the GCP services you would use and justify your choices.
keyPoints: Cloud SQL or Cloud Spanner for relational database with managed backups
Cloud Storage for media file storage with CDN integration
BigQuery or Pub/Sub for real-time analytics pipeline
Cloud Load Balancing with Cloud CDN for autoscaling and performance

questions.question: Explain Google Cloud's AI/ML offerings. Discuss the differences between AI Platform, AutoML, and pre-trained Vision/NLP APIs. How would you approach building a custom machine learning model on GCP for image classification, considering data preprocessing, training, deployment, and inference?
keyPoints: AI Platform: managed training/serving for custom models
AutoML: no-code ML for limited datasets
Vision/NLP APIs: pre-trained models for common use cases
Custom ML workflow: Cloud Storage for data, AI Platform for training, AI Platform Prediction for deployment
